# User Story: Rich Media Summaries for Briefings

**Status:** ⬜ Backlog

## Summary
As a user, I want a week-at-a-glance image plus a short caption for my briefings so that I can quickly scan the week without reading a long message.

---

## Background

**Tier 3 — Nice to have.** Briefings are generated by `remy/scheduler/briefings/` (morning, afternoon, evening, retrospective) and sent as text (or Markdown) by the proactive scheduler. A “week-at-a-glance” visual (e.g. a single image: calendar strip, goal checklist, or summary stats) with a one-line caption would make it easier to absorb at a glance and could be shared or saved.

Relevant code: `remy/scheduler/briefings/` (e.g. `MorningBriefingGenerator`, `MonthlyRetrospectiveGenerator`), `remy/scheduler/proactive.py` (sending briefings), and any existing image generation or templating.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Week-at-a-glance asset.** A briefing variant (e.g. weekly summary or enhanced morning briefing) can produce a “week-at-a-glance” representation: one image (e.g. PNG) that summarises the week (e.g. key dates, goals, or metrics). Exact layout is implementation-defined (e.g. simple calendar strip, list of goals, or combined).
2. **Caption.** The same briefing sends a short caption (e.g. one or two sentences) that can be used as Telegram caption or alt text.
3. **Delivery.** The image is sent to the user’s primary chat (same as existing briefings) with the caption; if image generation fails, fall back to text-only briefing.
4. **Optional scope.** Initially this can apply to one briefing type (e.g. weekly summary or Monday morning “week ahead”); other briefings remain text-only unless extended later.
5. **No regression.** Existing text briefings (morning, afternoon, evening, retrospective) continue to work; rich media is additive.

---

## Implementation

**Files:** New or existing module under `remy/scheduler/briefings/` (e.g. `week_at_a_glance.py` or extension of an existing generator), image generation utility (e.g. PIL/Pillow or reportlab for a simple layout), `remy/scheduler/proactive.py` (send photo + caption).

### Image generation

- Use Pillow (PIL) or similar to produce a single image: e.g. 800×600 or 1024×768. Content: week dates (e.g. Mon 2 Mar – Sun 8 Mar), optional list of goals or events, optional one-line summary. No external service required for MVP; keep layout simple.
- Alternatively, generate an HTML fragment and render to image (e.g. imgkit or weasyprint) if the team prefers; document dependency and fallback.
- If image generation raises, log and return None; caller sends text-only.

### Caption

- Build a one- or two-sentence summary from the same data (e.g. “Week of 2–8 Mar: 3 goals active, 5 calendar events. Focus: project X.”). Use as `caption` in `send_photo`.

### Scheduler integration

- Add a job (e.g. weekly on Monday morning) or extend an existing briefing to: (1) build week-at-a-glance data (goals, calendar for the week), (2) call the image generator, (3) if image OK, send with caption; else send text summary only. Reuse primary chat and allowed-users logic from existing briefing jobs.

### Notes

- Keep image size and complexity modest to avoid timeouts and dependency bloat.
- Consider accessibility: caption should convey the same key information as the image.

---

## Test Cases

| Scenario | Expected |
|----------|----------|
| Week-at-a-glance job runs, image generated | User receives one image with caption in primary chat |
| Image generation fails | User receives text-only summary; no crash |
| No primary chat / no allowed users | Same as other briefings: skip send, log |
| Caption length | Within Telegram caption limit (1024 chars); truncate if needed |

---

## Out of Scope

- Multiple images per briefing (carousel).
- User-customisable layout or branding.
- Video or other media types.
- Rich media for all briefing types in this story (only one type required; others can be follow-up).
